# <Title>

<!-- Copy this for any authored doc that isn't a PRD or an ADR.

     Before writing:
     - `id` must be unique. Check with:  grep -rh '^id: ' docs/ | sort | uniq -d
     - `type` must come from the list in docs/README-DOCS.md §4
     - `tags` must come from the 15-tag controlled vocabulary in §3 — if none fit, amend
       README-DOCS first and log it in the amendment block. Never invent a tag inline.
     - `title` and `description` are required: the board renders them, and a missing title
       falls back to the first H1, which can land inside a code fence.
     - `links` is where the value compounds. A doc that links to nothing is a dead end. -->

     and who is asking it? If you can't write this, the doc doesn't know what it's for. -->

<!-- Optional closers, use what applies:

     ## Known gaps
     State them plainly. A doc that hides what it doesn't cover gets trusted too much.

     ## Open questions
     Use TODO(james) markers inline so they can be grepped:
     grep -rn "TODO(james)" docs/

     ## Honesty tags
     If reconstructing from code rather than from record, tag claims
     [intended] / [inferred] / [unknown]. Ask rather than invent. -->
